data.title: Santos makes Alaska oil discovery, begins development planning
data.description:
Santos (STOSF) (SSLZY) said Wednesday it successfully completed the Quokka-1 appraisal well, prompting plans for the development of an oil project that could mirror the production capacity of its Pikka Phase 1 operation.

The company said Quokka-1 encountered a high-quality reservoir with 143 ft of net oil pay in the Nanushuk formation, and an initial test achieved a flow rate of 2,190 bbl/day of oil.

"Located strategically to the east of our Pikka phase 1 development, Quokka represents another high-return opportunity that strengthens our position on the North Slope and extends our development runway in Alaska for years to come," Managing Director Kevin Gallagher said.

Santos (STOSF) (SSLZY) controls a 51% operating interest in the unit, with Repsol (REPYF) (REPYY) controlling the remaining 49%.

data.title: Deutsche Bank raises Chairman Alexander Wynaendts' annual pay
data.description:
Deutsche Bank (DB) has increased the fixed annual basic compensation for the board chairman, Alexander Wynaendts, to €1.15M from €950K.

Furthermore, the German banking group said the person chairing a supervisory board committee will generally receive additional remuneration in the future, and previous exemptions will no longer apply.

The supervisory board members will see their compensation raised to €350K from €300K, while the pay will hike to €550K from €475K for the deputy chairman.

Additionally, Deutsche said Frank Witter is set to step down from the board, effective from the close of the annual general meeting on May 28, for personal reasons.

Carsten Knobel has been proposed for election to the supervisory board.

The bank is set to hold its AGM in person for the first time since 2019.

Furthermore, the board has recommended a dividend of €1.00 per share for 2025, up ~50% from €0.68 per share paid in 2024.

Additionally, Deutsche had launched a €1.0B share buyback program in February 2026, which increases the cumulative capital distributions for the financial years 2021-2025 to €8.5B. The capital distributions exceed the bank's original target of €8.0B.

data.title: Twilio set to gain for seven straight sessions
data.description:
Twilio (TWO) shares are set to gain for seven straight sessions, as the stock is up over 3% during afternoon trading on Wednesday.

The communications software company’s shares gained nearly 9.6% in the preceding six sessions. They have also risen nearly 10% over the past month.  

Earlier this week, Jefferies upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold, raising its price target to $160 from $125.

"We are upgrading TWLO to Buy as we have greater conviction in the role TWLO will play in the Voice AI tech stack. Further, even a modest increase in traction can serve as a sustainable tailwind for gross profit growth for years to come and drive upside to consensus," said analyst Samad Samana.

The analyst believes that the combination of greater relevance in an agentic AI world plus better fundamentals will result in a better valuation multiple as well.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 21 out of 29 analysts rated TWLO a Buy and above, six analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and two recommended Sell.

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s quant rating, TWLO has a Buy rating with a score of 3.70 out of 5. The company has been graded B for growth, profitability, and momentum but is weighed down by a C- grade for valuation and revisions.

However, Seeking Alpha analysts are cautious, rating it a Hold.

On a year-to-date basis, TWLO shares have fallen 4%, underperforming the broader market, which has declined 1%.

data.title: Meta introduces Muse Spark AI model, aimed at products
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) introduced its Muse Spark AI model on Thursday, the first model from its Superintelligence group and one aimed at products.

“Muse Spark is the first model in our new Muse series — a deliberate and scientific approach to model scaling where each generation validates and builds on the last before we go bigger,” the company said in a blog post. “This initial model is small and fast by design, yet capable enough to reason through complex questions in science, math, and health. It is a powerful foundation, and the next generation is already in development. Muse Spark now powers the Meta AI assistant in the Meta AI app and meta.ai, built to support complex reasoning and multimodal tasks.”

Meta said Muse Spark is built specifically for its products and will be able to power a smarter and faster Meta AI. In addition, it will also be able to incorporate new features that cite recommendations and content people share across Instagram, Facebook, and Threads, Meta added.

Currently being used to power the Meta AI app and website, Muse Park will come to WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, Messenger, and AI glasses in the “coming weeks,” Meta added.

It will also be made available in API to “select partners,” Meta said.

data.title: Freshpet's moat can withstand threats from Costco's Kirkland and The Farmer's Dog -- Cowen
data.description:
Recent developments in the pet food sector are not likely to be as detrimental to Freshpet (FRPT) as the share price indicates, and the company can actually take share from legacy dry kibble and wet food formats. With one of the “most formidable competitive moats in consumer products” and the stock priced attractively after a 17% retracement over the last 30 days, TD Cowen views Freshpet (FPRT) as a Buy and set an $80 target price that assumes 27% upside from Tuesday’s closing price.

“Concerns about increased competition from Costco’s new private label entry and Farmer’s Dog expansion on Walmart.com will prove overdone,” said TD Cowen’s Robert Moskow, adding that Freshpet (FRPT) has “sufficient scale and competitive advantages to withstand threats like these.”

With Costco (COST) doubling the size of its pet food refrigerators in 60% to make room for both its Kirkland brand and Freshpet (FRPT), Moskow believes the latter will benefit from not only the incremental shelf space (which will more than offset losses to Kirkland) but also the limited introduction of the Kirkland brand to 40% of Costco (COST) stores .

Admittedly, The Farmer’s Dog is the leading direct-to-consumer fresh dog food brand. But the brand will sell its merchandise at a 35% price premium at Walmart.com compared to its website. As a result, Moskow believes that the price difference and Walmart’s (WMT) plan to not sell The Farmer’s Dog at its brick-and-mortar stores do not pose a risk to Freshpet’s (FRPT) Walmart sales.

Freshpet (FRPT) shares are higher for a second straight day and have recovered losses associated with The Farmer’s Dog placement on Walmart.com (WMT).

data.title: Ichor Holdings tops Seeking Alpha's quant rankings among small-cap tech stocks ahead of Q1 earnings
data.description:
The technology sector had a turbulent start to the year, with a broad selloff compounded by pressures from the U.S.–Iran conflict and broader global market weakness.

The State Street Technology Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLK) fell 7.90% in the first quarter, underperforming the wider market, which declined 4.81% over the same period.

As companies prepare to report their results for the quarter gone by, FactSet analyst John Butters noted that 59 out of 110 companies have issued positive EPS guidance, the highest in five years. The majority of these positive earnings projections were concentrated in the information technology and energy sectors.

The tech sector stood out with the highest number of companies issuing positive EPS guidance. Overall, 33 tech firms projected a positive EPS for the quarter.

“This quarter ties the mark with the previous quarter for the second-highest number of companies in the information technology sector issuing positive EPS guidance for a quarter since FactSet began tracking this metric in 2006. The current record is 36, which occurred in Q3 2025,” Butters said.

Within the information technology sector, the semiconductors and semiconductor equipment industry leads in positive EPS guidance, with 10 companies issuing upbeat forecasts.

Mirroring EPS guidance trends, the information technology sector leads all 11 sectors with 47 companies issuing positive revenue guidance. This also marks a record high for the sector since FactSet began tracking the metric in 2006, surpassing the previous peak of 45 in Q2 2021.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating system, the sector has an average health score of 3.12. The system awards grades based on quantitative measures, like valuation, earnings growth, and recent stock performance. The highest possible score for any individual company is a 5.

Among individual companies in the tech sector, 47 stocks were rated as Buy or higher based on their Quant ratings, 200 stocks were rated Neutral, and 33 stocks were considered Sell or lower.

The following is a list of five of the top- and bottom-rated small-cap tech stocks with their quant ratings ahead of the upcoming earnings:

Top five stocks

Ichor Holdings (ICHR); Quant Rating: 4.98

POET Technologies (POET); Quant Rating: 4.83

Ituran Location and Control (ITRN); Quant Rating: 4.80

Mitek Systems (MITK); Quant Rating: 4.79

JinkoSolar Holding (JKS); Quant Rating: 4.73

Bottom five stocks

Forward Industries (FWDI); Quant Rating: 1.03

PAR Technology (PAR); Quant Rating: 1.04

C3.ai (AI); Quant Rating: 1.10

SEALSQ (LAES); Quant Rating: 1.14

Rapid7 (RPD); Quant Rating: 1.15

data.title: Wall Street rallies as the U.S. and Iran agree to a ceasefire with conditions
data.description:
Wall Street rallied Wednesday after President Donald Trump agreed to pause planned strikes on Iran for two weeks, contingent on the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) surged +2.9%, while the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) popped +2.4%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) climbed +2.6%. 

On a sector-by-sector basis, ten of the 11 S&P segments were up in the green, with communication services at the top. At the other end of the spectrum, energy has been the weakest performer, with oil (CL1:COM) down more than 15%. 

Market sentiment got a massive boost as tensions between the U.S. and Iran seemed to ease. Furthermore, volatility eased notably on Wednesday as the CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) dropped below 20, marking its lowest reading since February 27. 

Trump said the U.S. has received a 10-point proposal from Iran, and it is “a workable basis on which to negotiate.” The ceasefire remained contingent on Iran agreeing to an opening of the Strait of Hormuz. 

Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has announced Iran will agree to a ceasefire "if attacks against Iran are halted." 

U.S. Treasury yields slipped across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dipped 2 basis points to 4.27%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) moved down 3 basis points to 3.77%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) edged lower by 2 basis points to 4.86%. 

“Wall Street is exhaling as President Trump pressed pause on the destruction button, as there are enough signs that there's a will to negotiate a deal, and that optimism is helping to end this stock market correction,” Robert Edwards, chief investment officer of Edwards Asset Management, said. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Southwest Airlines Company (LUV) climbed +6.1%, while shares of Occidental Petroleum (OXY)  dropped -6.5%.

data.title: Roku set for seven straight sessions of gains
data.description:
Roku (ROKU) shares are on track for a seventh straight session of gains on Wednesday, as the stock rose over 3% to $101.87 in afternoon trading.

The San Jose, California-based company gained about 12% in the preceding six sessions. Overall, the stock has lost 6% so far this year, compared to the over 3% fall in the broader S&P 500 Index.

ROKU is up nearly 5% over the past one month. The stock closed marginally higher on Tuesday at $98.53.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, ROKU has a Hold rating with a score of 3.37 out of 5. The company received an A in the prospect of growth, while it got a D- in the valuation factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 24 analysts gave the TV streaming company a Buy or above, five analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and no one recommended Sell or lower. Seeking Alpha analysts are also bullish and see the stock as a Buy.

Earlier in February, Wall Street analysts praised Roku's fourth-quarter results and highlighted multiple catalysts for growth at the company going into 2026.

BofA said Roku is expected to benefit from various favorable industry trends along with certain company-specific actions, which include growth in CTV advertising, secular growth in streaming video domestically and internationally, and improving fill rates in the advertising business.

“Roku demonstrates accelerating top-line growth and robust profitability, with management prioritizing sustained margin expansion and operational leverage,” noted Seeking Alpha analyst Julian Lin.

Roku, in March, said it has launched its affordable ad-free SVOD streaming service, Howdy, on Amazon's Prime Video.

data.title: What’s next for Tesla after a disappointing EV delivery report
data.description:
What is next for Tesla after a disappointing Q1 2026 report?

Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has faced a challenging start to 2026, with shares falling approximately 21.5% year to date as of early April. The selloff was accelerated by a disappointing first-quarter production and delivery report, which revealed a significant inventory build of 50,363 vehicles and a dramatic 38.9% miss in energy storage expectations.

Still, while the immediate reaction to the quarterly figures has been negative, analysts remain divided on whether the current valuation represents a buying opportunity or a warning sign of further declines.

What Do Seeking Alpha Analysts Say About Tesla’s Future?

Bulls pointed to the long-term “optionality” inherent in Tesla’s non-automotive ventures, specifically highlighting progress in Full Self-Driving (FSD), the Robotaxi initiative, and the Optimus robot. They also noted pockets of international growth, such as untapped demand catalysts in South Korea, and suggested that the risk-reward ratio is becoming favorable as the stock becomes “cheap” relative to its future potential in AI and energy.

Bears, however, highlighted structural issues in the core automotive business, including narrowing competitive moats and intensifying pressure from rivals like BYD (BYDDF). They pointed to the massive discrepancy between production and deliveries, leading to a concerning rise in inventory, and argued that a trailing P/E ratio of over 300x is unjustifiable given the current margin compression and brand deterioration.

Here’s a breakdown of what some analysts had to say:

  • The Techie, Seeking Alpha Analyst, Rating: Buy: “Tesla stock is experiencing a sharp selloff, down 21.5% YTD, as the broader market pulls back on geopolitical uncertainty. I’m upgrading Tesla to a cautious Buy at current levels and think the risk-reward is favorable if the geopolitical and tariff headwinds ease.” - Tesla Stock: It’s Cheap Again (Rating Upgrade)

  • Amrita Roy, Investing Group Leader, Rating: Buy: “Tesla faces near-term headwinds in its core automotive segment, but I see 2026 as a ‘prove it’ year for its AI and robotics vision… I plan to add to TSLA shares at $330 and $273, maintaining an underweight portfolio allocation, as I expect short-term volatility, but not to the degree that Wall Street analysts suggest.” - Tesla Won’t Crash Anytime Soon

  • Forward Analytics, Financial Analyst, Rating: Sell: “TSLA faces structural delivery declines, margin compression, brand deterioration, and intensifying competition, notably from BYD… My DCF scenarios project 14% to 76% downside from current levels, even under optimistic assumptions.” - Tesla: Three Scenarios, Zero Upside

  • Bill Maurer, Market Enthusiast, Rating: Bearish: “TSLA’s valuation remains extremely high at 183x 2026 earnings, far exceeding both traditional automakers and tech giants… the energy storage business fell dramatically short of analyst expectations.” - Tesla: Weak Start To 2026

  • HF Analyst, Equity Research, Rating: Bearish: “Inventory build is getting worse with 50,363 vehicles… TSLA’s competitive EV moat has narrowed, raising questions about its long-term market dominance and valuation.” - Tesla Q1 2026 Deliveries Strengthen The Bearish Outlook

What Do the Quant Ratings Say?

Tesla currently carries a cautious rating from Seeking Alpha’s quant system, reflecting the disconnect between its high-growth narrative and recent operational misses. While the company retains its status as a tech leader, its quantitative scores have been weighed down by delivery declines and valuation concerns.

Valuation: The company receives a poor grade in valuation, with a trailing P/E ratio of 335.12x that far exceeds the sector median and traditional automotive peers.

Growth: Growth metrics have come under pressure, receiving a low grade following a Q1 2026 delivery report that showed vehicle deliveries falling well below analyst expectations.

Profitability: Despite margin compression, Tesla maintains a relatively strong grade in profitability compared to the sector median, though analysts warn this is at risk due to ongoing demand-stimulating discounts.

Momentum: Momentum carries a weak grade, as the stock has plummeted 21.5% YTD, severely underperforming the S&P 500 and other members of the “Mag 7.”

Earnings Revisions: The company is receiving a downward revision grade, as analysts lower Q1 and full-year 2026 estimates following the weak energy storage and delivery results.

What’s the Latest News on Tesla?

Tesla (TSLA) recently released its Q1 2026 production and delivery report, which has served as a primary catalyst for recent bearish sentiment. The report revealed that production significantly exceeded deliveries, leading to an inventory surplus of 50,363 vehicles. Furthermore, the company’s energy storage business, a key pillar of its non-automotive growth story, missed analyst estimates by a staggering 38.9%.

The company is also grappling with intensifying competition in the electric vehicle space, particularly from BYD (BYDDY), which continues to challenge Tesla’s market share. This competitive pressure, combined with expiring EV incentives and geopolitical uncertainty involving tariffs, has forced Tesla to utilize demand-stimulating discounts that threaten its long-term margin profile.

Looking ahead, investors are focusing on the company’s “prove it” year in AI and robotics. While core automotive metrics are struggling, the market is closely watching for tangible progress in the Robotaxi and Optimus programs, as these segments are increasingly viewed as the primary justification for the company’s $1.35 trillion market capitalization.
